programme.desc: We introduce the renewed NEWSROOM TOKYO featuring its team of new anchors and highlighting its appeal through a special Expo-focused episode.

programme.desc: Searching for Beauty in the Night Sea Photographer Otani Sho discusses being the first Japanese to be named Nature Photographer of the Year by the International Photography Awards and his passion for photographing small sea creatures.
programme.title: Direct Talk

programme.desc: A Surge in Coal Amid an Energy Crisis The US-Iran conflict led to what the IEA called the worst-ever energy crisis, and more power demand was predicted with the summer heat. How do the crisis and a surge in coal use affect climate change?
programme.title: IMPACTS:Climates Change the World

programme.desc: The Niwaka Costume Festival The filmmakers shed light on the Edo townspeople's affinity for cosplay as shown in an artwork where a high-ranking courtesan tends to a patron while a jester attempts to entertain with an eccentric costume.
programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-Life

programme.desc: GLP-1 Drugs for Obesity: Benefits and Risks The filmmakers explore the impact of GLP-1 receptor agonists on obesity management, while highlighting the significant risks associated with unsupervised use and the wider therapeutic potential of these treatments.
programme.title: Medical Frontiers

programme.desc: The host sheds light on various aspects of Japan that demonstrate the country's uniqueness and innovation in a wide range of fields.
programme.title: Catch Japan

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: A New Look at Shadows The hosts explore innovative ways artists use shadows in art and design to change how people see the world.
programme.title: Design X Stories

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: Individuals from all walks of life meet at a burial site under cherry trees to share their stories, discussing death and becoming "grave friends."
programme.title: Grave Friends

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: The Battle of Journalists in Bangladesh Bangladeshi journalists aim to convey the truth and regain the trust of the people after 15 years of strict censorship under the Hasina administration.
programme.title: Asia Insight

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: I Was Born in Japan and Called Gaijin Chuk Besher, who was born and raised in Japan, talks about being called a gaijin or an outsider, which prompts him to ask questions about identity and diversity.
programme.title: Chatroom Japan

programme.desc: Cosmetic Sponges Experts talk about the evolution of cosmetic sponges and how they are manufactured to achieve their specific capabilities.
programme.title: Japan's Top Inventions

programme.desc: Japanophiles: Johan Nordstrom Johan Nordstrom, a film scholar from Sweden, discusses century-old movies and Japan's unique tradition of live narration for silent films.
programme.title: Japanology Plus

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: Bon Odori: Rhythm and Connection The filmmakers explore how the Bon Odori dance serves as a global medium that brings together individuals from diverse cultural backgrounds.
programme.title: NEO-JAPONISM

programme.desc: S01 E02 — Kishu The filmmakers shed light on the eco-friendly charcoal production in Kishu, Japan, highlighting the process of cutting down suitable trees while promoting forest regrowth.
programme.title: A Culture Built with Wood

programme.title: Ramen Japan

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-Life

programme.desc: Big Changes in the Noodle World! The filmmakers explore the changes in Japan's ramen and udon eateries, including takeovers by food-service giants, by going behind the curtain of the industry.

programme.desc: A Surge in Coal Amid an Energy Crisis The US-Iran conflict led to what the IEA called the worst-ever energy crisis, and more power demand was predicted with the summer heat. How do the crisis and a surge in coal use affect climate change?

programme.desc: An EWTN original documentary presents the life, missionary journeys and legacy of the apostle St. Paul, who was driven by a zealous love of Christ to proclaim the Gospel worldwide.
programme.title: St. Paul the Apostle: Burning for Christ

programme.desc: S01 E07 — St. Robert Bellarmine, Pt. 1 Dr. Matthew Bunson explores the lives of the Doctors of the Church, including St. Augustine, St. Francis de Sales, and St. Teresa of Avila; during a time of great division, Robert Bellarmine defended Church.
programme.title: The Doctors of the Church

programme.desc: S01 E02 — Lindsey The COVID-19 pandemic through the eyes of a nurse at the Lourdes shrine; Lindsey Mooney gives hope and consolation to those who have lost loved ones and those who experienced the hardships of the disease themselves.
programme.title: My Lourdes Faith Journey

Tragedy follows when a new-to-town, troublemaking teen meets other anguished children of feckless parents.
programme.title: Rebel Without a Cause

programme.desc: Clumsy Inspector Jacques Clouseau (Peter Sellers) chases a jewel thief (David Niven) and his nephew (Robert Wagner).
programme.title: The Pink Panther

programme.desc: Friends (Guy Madison, Kim Novak, Brian Keith) join a college student's scheme to rob a Reno casino for kicks.
programme.title: 5 Against the House

programme.desc: A singing comedian (Dean Martin) flops on his own, then soars with a sidekick (Jerry Lewis) but insists on hogging the show.
programme.title: The Stooge

programme.desc: Bungling Inspector Clouseau (Peter Sellers) once again seeks the Pink Panther diamond and maddens his boss (Herbert Lom).
programme.title: The Return of the Pink Panther

programme.desc: A gruff neighbor (Lee Marvin) and a newspaper editor (Gary Merrill) help a teenage farmer (Brandon de Wilde) grow up in circa-1910 Missouri.
programme.title: The Missouri Traveler

programme.desc: Secret agent Matt Helm (Dean Martin) and a blonde (Sharon Tate) track $1 billion in gold in the last of four Matt Helm movies.
programme.title: The Wrecking Crew

programme.desc: A man in a chair (James Cagney) comments as, one by one, characters enter Nick's (William Bendix) San Francisco bar.
programme.title: The Time of Your Life

programme.desc: The Caboose Story Steve must uncover whether an old girlfriend, who turned out to be a dangerous spy, is behind the many recent acts of sabotage in Japan.
programme.title: Dangerous Assignment

programme.desc: S02 E09 — The Bride A racket swindles an immigrant out of his life savings.
programme.title: Man With a Camera

programme.desc: S01 E19 — Blondie Redecorates Dagwood hates the way the living room looks and wants it redecorated, but deciding who should design it, and in what style, complicates matters.
programme.title: Blondie

programme.desc: S06 E08 — Lucy and Robert Goulet Lucy tries to enter a truck driver in a Robert Goulet "look-alike" contest.
programme.title: The Lucy Show

programme.desc: S01 E31 — Dark Star Little Joe falls for a girl who has been disowned by her people because they think she is a witch and, therefore, an outcast.
programme.title: Bonanza

programme.desc: Two buddies win a car and head for Hollywood to meet Anita Ekberg.
programme.title: Hollywood or Bust

programme.desc: Doc Holliday (Kirk Douglas) joins Wyatt Earp (Burt Lancaster) and his brothers for a Tombstone showdown with the Clanton gang.
programme.title: Gunfight at the O.K. Corral

programme.desc: A detective (Peter O'Toole) helps a French art forger's daughter (Audrey Hepburn) steal a fake Venus.
programme.title: How to Steal a Million

programme.desc: Secret agent Matt Helm (Dean Martin) and a blonde (Sharon Tate) track $1 billion in gold in the last of four Matt Helm movies.
programme.title: The Wrecking Crew

programme.desc: Poor white tenant farmers (Zachary Scott, Betty Field, Beulah Bondi) battle fierce odds to make a living on a Texas plot.
programme.title: The Southerner

programme.desc: Blue-collar buddies (Sidney Poitier, Bill Cosby) search the underworld for a winning lottery ticket lost in a nightclub holdup.
programme.title: Uptown Saturday Night

programme.desc: Horrible old movies hosted by Dr. Paul Bearer direct from his home, the Tenement Castle.
programme.title: Dr. Paul Bearer's Creature Feature

programme.desc: S01 E31 — Dark Star Little Joe falls for a girl who has been disowned by her people because they think she is a witch and, therefore, an outcast.
programme.title: Bonanza

programme.desc: Banned from racing, a brash young driver (Mickey Rooney) returns to Indianapolis and burns up the track.
programme.title: The Big Wheel

programme.desc: A forest ranger (Christopher George) tries heavy firepower on a very big Georgia bear loose in a national park.
programme.title: Grizzly

programme.desc: A disturbed girl (Anne Baxter) with a heart condition moves in with her doctor and flirts with his married brother (Ralph Bellamy).
programme.title: Guest in the House

programme.desc: A doctor (John Forsythe) sets an expiration date with a rich patient's (Richard Kiley) wife (Reta Shaw).
programme.title: Murder Once Removed

programme.desc: The Tabernacle Choir at Temple Square performs songs of hope, faith and inspiration; singing sacred hymns both new and old from around the world as well as uplifting selections from the American songbook.
programme.title: Music & the Spoken Word

programme.desc: A singing comedian (Dean Martin) flops on his own, then soars with a sidekick (Jerry Lewis) but insists on hogging the show.
programme.title: The Stooge

programme.desc: Clumsy Inspector Jacques Clouseau (Peter Sellers) chases a jewel thief (David Niven) and his nephew (Robert Wagner).
programme.title: The Pink Panther

programme.desc: Bungling Inspector Clouseau (Peter Sellers) once again seeks the Pink Panther diamond and maddens his boss (Herbert Lom).
programme.title: The Return of the Pink Panther

programme.desc: A novitiate leaves her convent and becomes governess to Capt. Von Trapp's seven children in Austria before World War II.
programme.title: The Sound of Music

programme.desc: For his fiancée's charity a stockbroker bets friends that for 24 hours he will tell only the truth.
programme.title: Nothing but the Truth

programme.desc: A songwriter (Dick Van Dyke) sees his big chance when rock 'n' roll idol Conrad Birdie visits Sweet Apple, Ohio, to give one last kiss to his biggest fan (Ann-Margret) before joining the Army.
programme.title: Bye Bye Birdie

programme.desc: A bachelor (Jerry Lewis) agrees to baby-sit for a Hollywood star (Marilyn Maxwell), who turns out to have triplets.
programme.title: Rock-a-Bye Baby

programme.desc: Friends, relatives and a lawyer (Moses Gunn) question the case of a ghetto basketball star slain by a white policeman.
programme.title: Cornbread, Earl and Me

programme.desc: The frontiersman (Bruce Bennett) leads settlers to Kentucky, where he makes peace with a Shawnee chief (Lon Chaney).
programme.title: Daniel Boone, Trail Blazer

programme.desc: When a dirty Scotland Yard cop is linked to a string of robberies across Europe, the agency asks a famous French detective (Alan Arkin) to help them find the culprits.
programme.title: Inspector Clouseau

programme.desc: An ex-convict (John Payne) is framed for a bank holdup done by a police captain (Preston Foster) and men wearing masks.
programme.title: Kansas City Confidential

programme.desc: When an outlaw (Lee Marvin) with a fake nose kills her father, a schoolmarm (Jane Fonda) hires his drunken twin to get revenge.
programme.title: Cat Ballou

programme.desc: Americans (Mark Stevens, Patric Knowles) try to smuggle gold from France to use against the British in the War of 1812.
programme.title: Mutiny

programme.desc: A couple (Dick Van Dyke, Debbie Reynolds) break up after 15 years of marriage, then get back together as the easy way out.
programme.title: Divorce American Style

programme.desc: White liberals must overcome their own latent racism when their strong-willed daughter announces her intention to marry a distinguished black physician.
programme.title: Guess Who's Coming to Dinner

programme.desc: During the Civil War, reports of a sea monster attacking ships alarm the Navy. An investigation finds the monster is a submarine, the "Nautilus," commanded by vengeance-seeking Capt. Nemo (Allen Holubar), who was once an Indian prince.
programme.title: 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea

programme.desc: Rock 'n' rollers (Chubby Checker, Gene Chandler, Vic Dana) put on a TV dance special.
programme.title: Don't Knock the Twist

programme.desc: A couple (Ron Howard, Cindy Williams) and their high-school friends do their own things on four mid-1960s New Year's Eves.
programme.title: More American Graffiti

programme.desc: A lusty Greek peasant (Anthony Quinn) shows a British writer (Alan Bates) how to live and run a lignite mine.
programme.title: Zorba the Greek

programme.desc: A tracker (Sammy Davis Jr.) helps a white rancher (Ernest Borgnine) avenge his son and rescue his daughter.
programme.title: The Trackers

programme.desc: A lone frontiersman (James Stewart) risks his life to forge peace between Arizona settlers and Cochise's (Jeff Chandler) Apache nation.
programme.title: Broken Arrow

programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes (Basil Rathbone) and Dr. Watson (Nigel Bruce) prowl foggy moors to save Sir Henry (Richard Greene) from a killer-dog family curse.
programme.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles
